title: Week of March 23, 2026
summary: This week focused on making OpsPal more reliable and accessible for RevOps teams. We added automated release notes generation to keep you informed of platform improvements, introduced revenue field discovery to better assess your Salesforce revenue operations setup, and improved Windows installation to make getting started easier. Several stability fixes ensure your automations run smoothly without interruption.

entries.headline: Salesforce automation tracking works more reliably
details: Improved tracking of automation changes in your Salesforce org
Better monitoring of flows, triggers, and classes during discovery

entries.headline: Production safety controls work better for read-only operations
details: Read-only operations like retrieving metadata no longer trigger unnecessary production warnings
Clearer distinction between safe investigation tasks and risky changes

entries.headline: Automated release notes keep you informed of platform updates
details: Weekly summaries automatically generated from development changes
Clear explanations of new features and improvements in plain language

entries.headline: Reduced false alarms from safety controls
details: Fixed six cases where legitimate operations were blocked unnecessarily
Better recognition of safe tasks versus risky changes

entries.headline: Simple record lookups work faster
details: Quick record verification queries run without extra safety delays
Single record lookups and small result sets process more efficiently

entries.headline: Windows installation now handles all dependencies automatically
details: Automatic installation of required tools on Windows computers
No manual setup steps for Node.js and other dependencies

entries.headline: Better error reporting when Salesforce connections fail
details: Clearer error messages when org authentication problems occur
Automatic validation of Salesforce connections before starting work

entries.headline: Windows installation script works with all PowerShell environments
details: Fixed compatibility issues with different PowerShell execution methods
More reliable installation process across Windows configurations

entries.headline: Read-only queries work on production Salesforce orgs
details: Safe data queries no longer blocked on production environments
Better balance between safety and functionality for investigation tasks

entries.headline: Revenue context saved in assessment results
details: Assessment findings now include revenue-related context for future reference

entries.headline: Customization framework preserves your settings through updates
details: Custom templates and branding survive platform upgrades
Organized storage for tenant-specific customizations

entries.headline: Revenue field discovery automatically finds your org's revenue setup
details: Automatically detects which fields track revenue in your Salesforce org
Identifies active sales processes and revenue metrics
Works with custom revenue fields beyond standard Amount

entries.headline: Windows setup prefers Git Bash for better compatibility
Setup process installs all required dependencies automatically
One-click Windows setup with PowerShell installer
Better setup experience with clearer instructions
Deployment safety tools prevent common Salesforce mistakes
details: Validates picklist and record type configurations before deployment
Checks field history limits to prevent tracking failures
Merges duplicate value settings automatically

entries.headline: Added automatic secret scanning to prevent credential leaks
Continuous security scanning prevents credential exposure
Removed hardcoded credentials for better security
Installation now downloads from official hosted location
Salesforce deployment validation works more reliably
details: Fixed three validation issues that caused deployment failures
Better logic analysis for flow decisions and field references
